question: The line chart shows sales over the years. Which statement best describes a limitation when interpreting this chart?

options.A: The chart is so small that it is difficult to distinguish precise year-by-year changes and exact values.
options.B: Sales clearly doubled every decade shown in the chart.
options.C: Sales in 1960 can be read precisely and compared accurately with 1970.
options.D: Sales remained exactly the same throughout the entire period.

explanation: Sales were roughly flat from 1960 to 1970, with rises before and after. Small font sizes can make it easy to misread the flat segment as a decline.
